pages.text: Every night, <name> loved sitting on her terrace in Pune, gazing at the bright moon. She’d ask Amma endless questions — “How do rockets fly? Does the Moon have gardens?” Amma laughed, packing warm aloo parathas for dinner. “One day, you’ll find out yourself, little astronaut.”

That night, as <name> watched the stars, something magical happened. A silver beam of light from the moon shimmered down, and whoooosh! — a tiny round spaceship floated onto the terrace! Out popped a friendly alien with green sparkly eyes. “Namaste, Earth friend! I’m Ziku. I need help!”

<name> blinked in surprise. “Help? From me?” Ziku nodded sadly. “Our Moon Crystal has cracked. Without it, the moonlight will fade.” Before she could even say “ISRO!”, Ziku handed her a small helmet. “Ready for lift-off?” he said. Together, they zoomed through the sky — ZOOM! SWOOSH! WHOA! — past twinkling stars and glowing comets.

On the Moon, <name> saw silver dust dancing in the air. “It’s beautiful!” she whispered. Ziku pointed to a crystal glowing faintly near a crater. “We must fix it before sunrise.” <name> remembered how Papa fixed her science project light at home. “Let’s use moon sand as glue!” she said.

Mixing shiny dust with gentle care, she pressed the crystal together. It flickered once, twice… and then — FLASH! — the Moon shone brighter than ever! Ziku cheered, spinning happily. “You did it, <name>! The Moonlight is safe!”

Back home, <name> woke up in her bed, moonlight spilling across her floor. On her pillow lay a small glowing pebble. She smiled. Maybe it was just a dream… or maybe not. That night, she wrote in her notebook: Lesson: “Curiosity takes you places — even to the Moon!”
